title: Joystick + Servo Control
subtitle: Control Motion with Your Hands!
overview: This session introduces students to joystick modules and servo motors using Arduino. Students will learn how joystick movement controls servo motor rotation and understand basic robotics control systems.

images._note: Add actual classroom photos, joystick module pictures, servo movement activities, and Arduino wiring diagrams.

keyTopics: Using Arduino IDE to Upload Code
Real-life Uses — Robots, Cranes, RC Cars

activity.title: Joystick Controlled Servo Motor
steps: Connect joystick GND to Arduino GND
Connect joystick VRx pin to A0
Connect servo signal pin to D9
Connect servo VCC and GND properly
Challenge: Control two servos using joystick X and Y axis

quiz.q: What does a joystick module do?
quiz.explanation: A joystick sends movement directions to the Arduino.

quiz.q: What type of motor is used in this project?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors rotate to specific angles precisely.

quiz.q: What is the rotation range of a standard servo motor?
quiz.explanation: Most servo motors rotate from 0° to 180°.

quiz.q: Which board acts as the brain of the project?
quiz.explanation: Arduino controls all components using code.

quiz.q: Which pin type reads joystick values?
quiz.explanation: Joystick outputs analog values read by analog pins.

quiz.q: Which Arduino pin is commonly used for servo signal?
quiz.explanation: D9 is commonly used for servo signal control.

quiz.q: What happens when the joystick moves?
quiz.explanation: Joystick movement changes servo motor angle.

quiz.q: What does VCC mean in electronics?
quiz.explanation: VCC provides power to the component.

quiz.q: Which software is used to upload Arduino code?
quiz.explanation: Arduino IDE is used to write and upload code.

quiz.q: Servo motors are commonly used in:
quiz.explanation: Servo motors are used in many robotics applications.

quiz.q: What kind of signal controls a servo motor?
quiz.explanation: Servo motors use PWM signals for angle control.
GND means ground connection in a circuit.

quiz.q: What happens if wiring is incorrect?
quiz.explanation: Proper wiring is important for the project to work.

quiz.q: What type of input does the joystick provide?
What robotics skill do students learn in this activity?
quiz.explanation: This project teaches important robotics concepts.
